question: In adults hospitalized with community-acquired pneumonia of unspecified organism (ICD-10 J18.9), what is the evidence and current guideline consensus on the necessity, optimal timing, and modality (chest X‑ray vs CT) for routine follow‑up imaging at ~6 weeks after clinical improvement, particularly regarding detection of underlying malignancy or chronic lung disease and identification of subgroups (e.g., smokers, age >50) who benefit most?

results.title: 2026 ICD-10-CM Diagnosis Code J18.9: Pneumonia, unspecified organism
results.snippet: The best preventive measures include washing your hands frequently, not smoking, and wearing a mask when cleaning dusty or moldy areas. There is a vaccine for pneumococcal pneumonia, a bacterial infection which accounts for up to a quarter of all pneumonias. ICD-10-CM J18.9 is grouped within Diagnostic Related Group(s) (MS-DRG v43.0):

results.title: ICD-10 Code for Pneumonia, unspecified organism- J18.9- Codify by AAPC
results.snippet: Code also: , if applicable, any associated condition such as: aspiration pneumonia (J69.-) ... Get crucial instructions for accurate ICD-10-CM J18.9 coding with all applicable Excludes 1 and Excludes 2 notes from the section level conveniently shown with each code. results.snippet: The code is valid during the current ... Unspecified diagnosis codes like J18.9 are acceptable when clinical information is unknown or not available about a particular condition ....

results.title: Pneumonia ICD-10 Codes: A Comprehensive Guideline
results.snippet: August 14, 2024 - The most common codes include J18.9 for unspecified pneumonia and J18.1 for lobar pneumonia. ... Community-acquired pneumonia is typically coded as J18.9 when the specific organism is not identified .

results.title: Pneumonia ICD-10 Code Guide: Coding & Reimbursement
results.snippet: April 9, 2025 - ICD-10 Code: J18.9 (Pneumonia, unspecified organism) In this case, J18.9 is appropriate as the organism has not been identified or confirmed . It’s important to document that the diagnosis remains uncertain and that additional tests are pending.

results.title: ICD-10 Code for Pneumonia, unspecified organism | J18.9 - Co
results.snippet: Do not use Pneumonia, unspecified organism if the clinician documents a specific pathogen (for example, "pneumococcal pneumonia," "Staphylococcus aureus pneumonia," or "influenza-associated pneumonia"). Instead, select the organism-specific ...

results.title: ICD Diagnosis Code J18.9: What It Is & When to Use
results.snippet: 2. Physical Examination: Auscultation reveals abnormal lung sounds, such as crackles or wheezing, which are consistent with pneumonia. 3. Radiological Evidence: Chest X-ray or CT scan shows infiltrates or consolidation in the lungs, supporting a diagnosis of pneumonia .

snapshot:
title: ICD-10-CM Diagnosis Code J18.9 - Pneumonia, unspecified organism
url: https://icdlist.com/icd-10/J18.9
meta: J18.9 is a billable diagnosis code used to specify pneumonia, unspecified organism. Synonyms: acute erosive gastritis, acute gastroenteritis, acute ulcerative
lead: J18.9 is a billable diagnosis code used to specify a medical diagnosis of pneumonia, unspecified organism. The code is valid during the current fiscal year for the submission of HIPAA-covered transactions from October 01, 2025 through September 30, 2026. Unspecified diagnosis codes like J18.9 are acceptable when clinical information is unknown or not available about a particular condition. Although a more specific c...
